stories.name: build-stamping-and-deploy-ordering
stories.title: Build version stamping and deploy ordering
stories.description: Inject a build-time version constant into the bundle and emit version.json into the static export root. In next.config.mjs, compute BUILD_VERSION via git rev-parse --short HEAD (falling back to Date.now()) using execSync from child_process, and expose it as process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION so it bakes into the client bundle. In the Makefile, define BUILD_VERSION with the same git-SHA/timestamp fallback, emit version.json into the LOCAL_DIST after the Next.js build completes, and split the deploy target into two sequential lftp sessions: first mirror everything except version.json (--exclude version.json), then upload version.json last with a serial put. No UI, no hook, no tests — the stamp is verified by the build output and the deploy ordering by manual inspection (MV-1, MV-2).

includes: app/next.config.mjs — import execSync from 'child_process'; compute BUILD_VERSION (git short SHA, fallback Date.now().toString()); expose via env: { NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION: BUILD_VERSION } in nextConfig

Makefile — define BUILD_VERSION := $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || date +%s); add post-build step writing {"version":"$(BUILD_VERSION)","builtAt":"<ISO>"} to $(LOCAL_DIST)/version.json; split deploy target: lftp mirror --exclude version.json first, then lftp put version.json last

stories.name: polling-hook-banner-i18n-and-tests
stories.title: Polling hook, update banner, i18n, app wiring, and tests
stories.description: Implement the full update-detection runtime: (1) Create app/src/hooks/useUpdateChecker.ts — a client-only hook that sets up a 5-minute interval to fetch /version.json?t=<ms> with { cache: 'no-store' }, triggers an immediate check on visibilitychange→visible and window focus events, compares fetched.version against the baked-in NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION, latches an updateAvailable boolean on first mismatch, and fails soft on all error paths (network error, non-200, parse failure) with no thrown errors and no console spam. (2) Create app/src/components/UpdateBanner.tsx — a non-blocking dismissible banner modelled on OfflineBanner.tsx (Chakra Alert + CloseButton), receiving updateAvailable as a prop, with data-testid attributes on root, reload button, and dismiss button; Reload calls window.location.reload(); dismiss sets a local dismissed flag (session-only, not persisted). (3) Extend app/src/lib/i18n.ts — add updateBanner: { message, reload, dismissAriaLabel } section to the Copy type and both en and de language objects; wire into the component via useCopy(). (4) Extend app/pages/_app.tsx — call useUpdateChecker() and render <UpdateBanner updateAvailable={...} /> in the app shell (client-only, no SSR concern). (5) Create app/tests/unit/useUpdateChecker.test.ts — unit tests covering version-comparison + latch logic and all fail-soft branches (mocking fetch via Object.defineProperty). (6) Create app/tests/e2e/update-detection.spec.ts — e2e tests using page.route('**/version.json**', ...) to intercept responses; covers: banner appears on version mismatch, no banner on same version, fail-soft on 404, dismiss is session-only (new version after dismiss re-shows banner), user can type and send with banner visible.

includes: app/src/hooks/useUpdateChecker.ts — NEW FILE; 5-minute interval fetch; visibilitychange + window focus triggers; cache: 'no-store' + ?t=<ms> cache-busting; latched updateAvailable boolean; fail-soft on all error paths

app/src/components/UpdateBanner.tsx — NEW FILE; non-blocking Chakra Alert; dismissible (session-only local state); data-testid='update-banner', 'update-banner-reload', 'update-banner-dismiss'; Reload → window.location.reload(); i18n via useCopy()
app/src/lib/i18n.ts — extend Copy type with updateBanner: { message: string; reload: string; dismissAriaLabel: string }; add en + de values
app/pages/_app.tsx — mount useUpdateChecker(); render <UpdateBanner updateAvailable={...} /> in app shell
app/tests/unit/useUpdateChecker.test.ts — NEW FILE; unit tests for comparison+latch logic and fail-soft branches
app/tests/e2e/update-detection.spec.ts — NEW FILE; e2e tests using page.route() interception; covers mismatch→banner, same-version→no-banner, 404→fail-soft, dismiss session-only, non-blocking typing
excludes: next.config.mjs version injection (S1 — NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION must be present before this story is meaningful in prod, but S2 can mock the env var in tests)
Makefile version.json emission and deploy ordering (S1)
Service worker / PWA layer (out of scope per spec §7)
Cross-tab BroadcastChannel coordination (out of scope per spec §7)
Auto-reload without user consent (out of scope per spec §7)

mock_dependencies: S1 — NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION env var must be set in the production bundle; unit and e2e tests mock it via process.env overrides or page.route() interception

test_fixtures.description: Playwright page.route() interceptor for /version.json — used to serve controlled version responses (mismatch, same, 404) in e2e tests without a real deploy. This is permanent test infrastructure for any future e2e test that needs to stage an update scenario.

dependency_notes: S1 must land before S2 reaches production — NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION and version.json must exist for the hook to have meaningful values to compare. S2 is independently implementable in isolation (unit tests mock the env var; e2e tests intercept /version.json via page.route()), but end-to-end functionality requires S1 to be deployed first.

seams.data: NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION baked into the client bundle at build time

invariants: NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION is a non-empty string at runtime
Its value equals the git short SHA when git is available, otherwise a numeric timestamp string
Its value is identical to the version field in the version.json emitted by the same build run

seams.data: version.json at the static site root, fetchable at /version.json

invariants: version is the comparison key — non-empty string, equals NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_VERSION from the same build run
builtAt is an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp string used only for diagnostics — equality decisions use version alone
version.json is uploaded to the server only after all hashed JS/CSS chunks and HTML files are fully in place (AC-DEPLOY-1)
